text: Would you like to {{c1::grab a drink}} sometime?
explanation: grab a drink is a casual, friendly way to invite someone to go out for a beverage (coffee, beer, or cocktails).
usage:
Pattern: grab a [item] (e.g. grab a coffee, grab a bite to eat, grab lunch)
  • Let's grab a coffee this afternoon.
  • Do you want to grab a bite to eat after work?
spanish: ¿Te gustaría ir a tomar algo alguna vez?

text: I had a great time tonight, we should {{c1::do this again}}.
explanation: do this again is a friendly, low-pressure phrase used at the end of a good social gathering or date to show interest in hanging out again.
usage:
Pattern: we should do this again [sometime/soon]
  • I really enjoyed the museum. We should do this again sometime!
  • It was great catching up. We should do this again soon.
spanish: La pasé genial esta noche, deberíamos repetir esto.
